overall.description: Gameplay footage from an open-world game (Red Dead Redemption 2) showing an eagle flying through a rural Western landscape. The bird soars under a railway bridge, over cliffs and grasslands, then follows a steam train through the countryside with snow-capped mountains visible in the distance.

events.description: eagle ascends from below bridge level to deck height, crossing over the railway structure

agents.ego_description: eagle soaring over river water with wings spread, approaching and ascending toward the railway bridge

entered.detail: long iron truss railway bridge spanning the river canyon
calm reflective water surface below the flight path
steep eroded cliff face with sparse vegetation
snow-capped mountain range on the horizon

segments.scene_description: The segment opens with an eagle flying low over a river toward a massive iron railway bridge. The bird ascends smoothly, transitioning from below the bridge to deck level, revealing the canyon walls and distant snow-capped mountains under bright midday sun.

scene_state.functional_zone: grassy cliffside transitioning to open plains

events.description: leaves the bridge structure behind, entering open grassland along the cliff edge

agents.ego_description: eagle banking left along the cliffside, maintaining altitude while leaving the river behind

entered.detail: rolling green grassland with scattered boulders
isolated oak trees dotting the landscape

segments.scene_description: Leaving the bridge behind, the eagle banks left along a grassy cliff edge. The terrain transitions from rocky canyon to open grassland with scattered trees, while the distant mountains remain visible ahead.

scene_state.functional_zone: rocky grassland with distant mountain views

events.description: steam train appears on railway tracks below, emitting black smoke

agents.ego_description: eagle gliding over rocky outcrops and grassy terrain, descending slightly toward the railway line

entered.detail: black steam locomotive pulling cargo cars with smoke stack emitting dark smoke
single track railway line cutting through the grassland

segments.scene_description: The eagle flies over rocky grassland formations with snow-capped mountains dominating the background. As the bird descends toward the valley floor, a steam train comes into view on the tracks below, marking the transition to the railway corridor.

scene_state.functional_zone: rural railway line with water tower and station

events.description: eagle adjusts flight path to follow parallel with the moving train

agents.ego_description: eagle flying alongside and slightly above the steam train, matching its speed through the countryside

notable_agents.action: steam locomotive moving along tracks emitting thick black smoke

entered.detail: tall wooden railway water tower with ladder and tank
metal windmill with spinning blades near the tracks
wooden platform with small building and cargo crates

segments.scene_description: The eagle follows the steam train as it chugs through the rural landscape, passing a wooden water tower and small station platform. The bird maintains pace with the locomotive, flying over grassy hills with a distant lake visible while the train emits a trail of black smoke.

merged_segments.scene_description: The shot follows a continuous aerial perspective as an eagle glides from a deep river canyon into an expansive rural valley. It begins with a low flight over a river, ascending smoothly to clear a massive iron railway bridge and reveal towering canyon walls and distant snow-capped mountains under a bright midday sun. As the bird leaves the bridge behind, the camera banks left along a grassy cliff edge, transitioning from the rocky canyon into open grasslands with scattered trees. The scene then shifts as the eagle descends from the rocky highlands toward the valley floor, where a steam train emerges on the tracks below. Finally, the camera maintains pace with the locomotive as it chugs past a wooden water tower and station platform, trailing black smoke across grassy hills toward a distant lake.
